name: FM Scanner
description: Live FM radio receiver with RDS display, frequency history, presets, and band scan. Streams audio from a remote site's RTL-SDR dongle (role=scanner) to the browser in real time.

name: Web SDR
description: Browser-based software defined radio with scrolling waterfall display. Tune any frequency within the capture bandwidth. WFM, NFM, and AM demodulation modes. Requires an RTL-SDR dongle on the client machine.

name: DAB Scanner
description: DAB digital radio browser. Scans all Band III channels to discover services, streams selected services as MP3 audio via welle-cli and ffmpeg. Displays DLS (Dynamic Label Segment) scrolling text. Requires welle-cli and ffmpeg on the client machine. v1.0.34: fix welle-cli left running after browser close — 30 s server-side session watchdog + beforeunload beacon; v1.0.33: fix -C/-D conflict on Pi when SDR serial is set (-T always; -C 1 only when no serial); v1.0.32: auto-detect Raspberry Pi and use -C 1 to limit CPU; v1.0.29: survive OFDM resync stalls on startup.

name: Logger
description: Compliance logger for continuous 24/7 recording of monitored streams. Hub mode aggregates recorded streams from all connected sites for remote playback — browse timelines and play audio from any site directly on the hub. Unified full-day seekable timeline, wall-clock timestamps, cross-block mark in/out, automatic segment stitching on export, auto-advance playback. Now-playing metadata integration: polls Planet Radio, Triton, or any JSON API every 30 s; falls back to DLS/RDS. Show-name band above timeline, per-song track band showing exact start/end times, track blocks highlighted in amber. v1.6.0: ffmpeg watchdog (kills hung processes after 30 s), gap detection with red timeline blocks and startup recovery, disk space alerts (pauses at <500 MB, warns at <5 GB), SHA-256 segment checksums, export audit log, per-stream silence threshold config, metadata_log auto-pruning, hub reconnect with exponential backoff. v1.6.1: watchdog countdown starts after first audio write (no spurious kills during DAB/slow-stream startup). v1.6.2: fix “Remote site not responding” after client site rename — evict stale catalog slugs on registration so renamed sites are immediately authoritative. Requires ffmpeg.

name: Meter Wall
description: Full-screen audio meter wall showing live PPM-style level bars for every monitored stream across all connected sites. Peak hold with decay at 5 Hz real-time update, LUFS-I readout, alert flash, RDS/DLS now-playing, site grouping, configurable grid density, auto-hiding fullscreen kiosk mode. Stereo streams show split L/R bars at 150 ms update rate.

name: Zetta Integration
description: RCS Zetta broadcast automation integration. Polls the Zetta SOAP service to show live now-playing data for each station — title, artist, cart number, category. Detects commercial/spot blocks in real time. Provides /api/zetta/status for chain and external integration. Includes WSDL discovery and a raw SOAP debug console. No extra pip packages required. v1.0.4: button loading states (shimmer while fetching), required attribute on URL field, spellcheck=false on URL input.

name: Morning Report
description: Daily morning briefing for broadcast engineers and non-technical staff. Auto-generates at 06:00 (configurable) covering the previous calendar day. Plain-English headline banner (all-clear or fault summary), per-chain health table with on-air %, traffic-light trend pills, human-readable outage durations, hourly fault heatmap, auto-detected notable patterns (clustering, above-average faults, recurring issues, clean streaks), and stream quality summary with translated labels. All configured chains appear even with no fault history. Hub-only. v1.2.3: Fixed Regenerate button doing nothing (missing helper functions).

name: Signal Path Latency
description: Signal path latency tracking for broadcast chains. Polls all connected sites for comparator delay measurements every 30 seconds, stores 90 days of history in SQLite, and computes rolling baselines. Displays per-comparator SVG sparklines, stable/drifting/alert status badges, and configurable alert thresholds per comparator pair. Hub-only.

name: Codec Monitor
description: Real-time connection monitor for broadcast contribution codecs — Comrex ACCESS/BRIC-Link, Tieline Gateway/ViA, Prodys Quantum ST, and APT/WorldCast Quantum. Polls each device via HTTP status-page scraping or SNMP and tracks connected/idle/offline state. Fires CODEC_FAULT and CODEC_RECOVERY alerts into the SignalScope Reports page on state changes. Mobile API at /api/mobile/codecs/status for the iOS app. Runs on hub and client nodes. v1.0.6: Save button loading state; required on Name/Host fields; 2-col form collapses to 1 col on mobile.

name: Push Server
description: Turns this SignalScope hub into a centralised push notification server for iOS and Android. Holds APNs (.p8 key) and FCM (service account JSON) credentials and delivers notifications on behalf of any SignalScope installation that points its Push Server URL here. Includes one-click migration from existing Settings credentials. Hub-only. v1.0.6: button loading states; credential fields get spellcheck=false/autocomplete=off; improved empty state for deliveries log; responsive APNs grid.

name: PTP Clock
description: Full-screen GPS-accurate wall clock for broadcast studios. Two display modes: digital (large HH:MM:SS with tenths) and analog studio clock (sweep second hand). Shows live PTP sync status, offset, and jitter. Custom branding via URL parameter. Time served from the GPS-disciplined server clock, accurate on any browser. v1.4.2: Save settings and Add preset buttons show shimmer loading state; preset rows wrap on narrow screens; mode grid collapses to 1 col on mobile.

name: Icecast Streaming
description: Manage Icecast2 streaming servers on client nodes. Re-streams any monitored input type (FM/RTL-SDR, DAB, ALSA, RTP, HTTP) to Icecast2 via the same PCM buffer tap used by the Logger plugin. Per-stream stereo toggle: HTTP inputs preserve native stereo; all others upmix mono to dual-mono. Hub overview shows live listener counts and stream status from all connected sites; create and manage streams on any client from the hub. Requires ffmpeg; Icecast2 installed separately. v1.1.1: all action buttons (Start/Stop/Save/Add/Delete) show shimmer loading state; passwords changed to type=password; responsive grid collapses on mobile; stream table gets horizontal scroll on small screens.

name: Listener
description: Live stream listener for presenters and producers. Shows all streams the user has access to as polished station cards with live level meters and one-tap audio playback. Stereo streams are indicated with a STEREO badge on the card and in the now-playing bar. Designed for non-technical users — auto-reconnects, animated equalizer, volume control, mobile-friendly. Hub-only.

name: Producer View
description: Simplified hub view for producers and presenters. Shows station status cards with live level indicators and a plain-English chain fault history. Reads directly from the fault log database (same source as Broadcast Chains) so clip play buttons always work, including clips uploaded asynchronously from remote client sites. Chain-filtered and site-filtered to only show what the user has permission to see. All-clear banner when everything is running normally. Integrates with the plugin-role user system (SignalScope 3.4.85+) — users assigned the Producer role go directly here on login. Hub-only. v1.4.2: Fix Sign out button broken for producer-role users (_ssConfirm undefined on pages without topnav — replaced with window.confirm).

name: AzuraCast
description: AzuraCast web radio integration. Polls configured AzuraCast servers for live now-playing data, listener counts, and station health. Station cards show current track, artist, progress bar, next track, live/AutoDJ status, and listener count. Fires AZURACAST_FAULT and AZURACAST_RECOVERY alerts when stations go offline or recover. Optionally cross-references with SignalScope silence detection — fires AZURACAST_SILENCE if a station is broadcasting but its linked input is silent. Hub overview aggregates all stations from all connected sites. v1.0.1: Discover button loading state; required on URL field; spellcheck=false on API key; improved empty states for stations and servers panels.

name: IP Link
description: Browser-based IP codec and SIP softphone. WebRTC rooms: hub creates named rooms with shareable talent URLs — contributors join from any browser with no app download, low-latency Opus audio, bidirectional IFB, live level meters, quality presets (Voice 64 kbps / Broadcast 128 kbps stereo / Hi-Fi 256 kbps stereo). SIP client: register with any SIP PBX supporting WebSocket transport (Asterisk, FreeSWITCH, 3CX); take and make SIP calls directly from the hub page; digest auth, incoming call banner with Answer/Decline, mic mute, RTT stats, persistent config. Hub-only. v1.1.23: fix root cause of all 'Invalid SDP line' errors — Python .strip() was removing the terminal \r\n from the stored offer; Chrome's line-oriented parser then failed on the unterminated last line, reporting whichever line that was as 'Invalid'; server now restores \r\n after strip; raw offer passed unchanged to setRemoteDescription (no JS SDP manipulation). SIP munge retained for outgoing INVITEs. v1.1.18: comprehensive SDP munge. v1.1.14: Safari/Chrome SDP compat.

name: Sync Capture
description: Multi-site synchronized audio capture. Select any combination of inputs from any connected sites, set a capture duration (5–300 s), and press Capture. The hub broadcasts a timestamped command; each client grabs the last N seconds from its rolling audio buffer at the agreed moment and uploads the clip. Hub-local inputs are captured directly. All clips are presented together on a single page with inline audio players for side-by-side listening and comparison — ideal for checking simulcast alignment, network contribution quality, or making reference recordings across a transmitter network. Hub-only. v1.0.13: atomic DB writes, upload size/validation guards, streaming clip serve, bounded dedup set, per-site command queue, EBU R128 LUFS + true peak, level-diff dB, sub-sample lag interpolation, octave-band spectrum, stereo L/R analysis, RDS/DLS metadata snapshot, BWF export, async alignment with job polling, single heapq scheduler, JS Map cache, ResizeObserver waveforms, reference selector, pagination + search, countdown timer, per-clip download buttons, configurable storage path. v1.0.18: DAW Session export — one-click ZIP download with all WAV clips plus REAPER (.rpp) and Adobe Audition (.sesx) session files; alignment offsets baked in when ⇌ Align has been run.
